audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:zeggen dat + subordinate clause]] and [[grammar:vragen of + subordinate clause]]. Use [[grammar:zeggen dat]] to report what someone said without quoting directly. Use [[grammar:vragen of]] to report a yes/no question.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:zeggen dat + subordinate clause]]: The complementizer [[grammar:dat]] introduces a subordinate clause with the finite verb at the end.; [[grammar:vragen of + subordinate clause]]: [[grammar:Of]] marks the embedded polar interrogative and sends the finite verb to clause-final position.

audienceSections.normal:
#### zeggen dat + subordinate clause

Use [[grammar:zeggen dat]] to report what someone said without quoting directly. Example: [[grammar:Sophie, de voorzitter, zegt dat de begroting klaar is.]]

#### vragen of + subordinate clause

Use [[grammar:vragen of]] to report a yes/no question. Example: [[grammar:Opa Henk vraagt of de subsidie is goedgekeurd.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### zeggen dat + subordinate clause

The complementizer [[grammar:dat]] introduces a subordinate clause with the finite verb at the end. Literal example: [[grammar:Sophie, de voorzitter, zegt dat de begroting klaar is.]]

#### vragen of + subordinate clause

[[grammar:Of]] marks the embedded polar interrogative and sends the finite verb to clause-final position. Literal example: [[grammar:Opa Henk vraagt of de subsidie is goedgekeurd.]]

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Zeggen dat]] reports a statement. [[grammar:Vragen of]] reports a yes/no question; both embedded clauses use subordinate word order.
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:Zeggen dat]] reports a statement. [[grammar:Vragen of]] reports a yes/no question; both embedded clauses use subordinate word order. The grammar IDs remain distinct and cumulative.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Fatima schrijft de notulen van de vergadering.]] — Fatima writes the minutes of the meeting.
- [[grammar:Sophie, de voorzitter, zegt dat de begroting klaar is.]] — Sophie, the chair, says that the budget is ready.
- [[grammar:Pieter meldt dat het centrum meer bezoekers heeft ontvangen.]] — Pieter reports that the center has received more visitors.
- [[grammar:Opa Henk vraagt of de subsidie is goedgekeurd.]] — Grandpa Henk asks whether the grant has been approved.
- [[grammar:Yasmin zegt dat de keuken volgende maand wordt vernieuwd.]] — Yasmin says that the kitchen will be renewed next month.
- [[grammar:Yasmin vraagt of vrijwilligers zich nog kunnen aanmelden.]] — Yasmin asks whether volunteers can still register.
- [[grammar:Sophie antwoordt dat de inschrijving open blijft.]] — Sophie answers that registration will remain open.
- [[grammar:Fatima legt elk besluit zorgvuldig vast.]] — Fatima carefully records every decision.
- [[grammar:Pieter bespreekt de bezoekersaantallen van het jaar ervoor.]] — Pieter discusses the visitor numbers from the previous year.
- [[grammar:Hij zegt dat er negenduizendnegenhonderdnegenennegentig bezoeken zijn geregistreerd.]] — He says that nine thousand nine hundred ninety-nine visits have been recorded.
- [[grammar:Opa Henk vraagt of de cijfers de dag ervoor zijn gecontroleerd.]] — Grandpa Henk asks whether the figures were checked the day before.
- [[grammar:Fatima antwoordt dat de gegevens twee keer zijn gecontroleerd.]] — Fatima answers that the data were checked twice.
- [[grammar:De volgende dag leest Sophie de samenvatting.]] — The following day Sophie reads the summary.
- [[grammar:Zij meldt dat alle besluiten in de notulen staan.]] — She reports that all decisions are in the minutes.
- [[grammar:Yasmin vraagt of iedereen een kopie ontvangt.]] — Yasmin asks whether everyone receives a copy.
- [[grammar:Fatima zegt dat de documenten die middag worden verstuurd.]] — Fatima says that the documents will be sent that afternoon.
